user_query:
We're doing a dry-run of our model router to prep a production switch. Please read these inputs and produce deterministic artifacts only from file logic (no external commands):

- input/router.config.json: contains prefix and alias routing plus default model
- input/router.schedule.json: contains timezone and schedule rules
- input/messages.jsonl: a batch of messages to route (one JSON object per line with id and text)
- input/times.csv: a list of timestamps to resolve against the schedule (columns: id,at)

Tasks:
1) Route messages by prefix and alias:
   - Parse each message’s leading prefix (e.g., @codex, @mini) and body.
   - Resolve aliases defined in the config (e.g., @m -> @mini, @c -> @codex).
   - Map to the model per prefixMap.
   - Status rules:
     - If no prefix → status = "no_prefix" and no model.
     - If prefix-only (no body after trimming) → status = "switch_only" and include model.
     - If unsupported prefix → status = "invalid_prefix" and no model.
     - Otherwise → status = "ok" and include model + fallbackModel.
   - Write to output/routes.json as: { "routes": [ { id, prefix, resolvedPrefix (if alias was used, otherwise same as prefix), body, status, model (or null), fallbackModel (or null) } ... ] }.

2) Resolve schedule for each timestamp in input/times.csv:
   - Treat all timestamps in the CSV as already expressed in the schedule timezone; do not convert timezones.
   - Consider only rules with enabled=true.
   - Start times are inclusive; end times are exclusive.
   - Overnight windows (e.g., 18:00->09:00) wrap across midnight and should match when the local clock time is >= start OR < end on the listed day.
   - If more than one rule matches, pick the one with the highest priority value.
   - If none match, return nulls.
   - Write to output/schedule_resolution.json as: { "resolutions": [ { id, at, ruleId (or null), model (or null) } ... ] }.

3) Create a deterministic audit log mirroring our router event semantics (one line per message):
   - For status "ok": { type: "route.success", prefix, resolvedPrefix, targetModel }
   - For status "switch_only": { type: "route.switch_only", prefix, resolvedPrefix, targetModel }
   - For status "no_prefix": { type: "route.skip", reason: "no_prefix" }
   - For status "invalid_prefix": { type: "route.failure", prefix, code: "INVALID_PREFIX", reason: "Unsupported prefix: <prefix>" }
   - Write each JSON object on its own line to output/audit_log.jsonl (no extra fields, no timestamps, deterministic content only).

4) Summarize counts:
   - Totals by status (ok, switch_only, no_prefix, invalid_prefix)
   - Counts by routed model for ok + switch_only statuses
   - Schedule resolution totals (count with a rule vs. no_rule)
   - Write to output/summary.json with keys: { total_messages, by_status, by_model, schedule: { with_rule, no_rule } }

Notes:
- Do not execute any CLI; infer everything from the input files.
- Trim bodies and preserve original prefixes in outputs; for aliases, also include resolvedPrefix.
- Use only the provided config and schedule.
- Write exactly to these files: output/routes.json, output/schedule_resolution.json, output/audit_log.jsonl, output/summary.json.
